meta.ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances & Pensions, Department of Administrative Reforms & Public Grievances (e-Governance Division)
meta.project_subject: Setting up of Project Monitoring Unit (PMU) for the works relating to the Implementation of recommendations of NesDA, 2021
meta.deliverables: The PMU will monitor and evaluate implementation of NeSDA 2021 recommendations for States/UTs/Central Governments. Key deliverables include: 1) Saturation of all 56 mandatory and optional e-services across state/UT governments, 2) Increased adoption of integrated centralized portals for e-service delivery, 3) Improvement in scores across all seven NeSDA assessment parameters, 4) Regular monitoring and analysis of NeSDA portal data including total e-services, mandatory services, integrated portals, auto appeal, and suo moto entitlement-based services, 5) Generation of regular reports and publications with baseline assessments, monitoring of central ministries/states/UTs on e-services delivery, identification of implementation bottlenecks, and dissemination of best practices, 6) Coordination with DARPG NIC team for portal monitoring and acting as POC for central ministries/states/UTs for portal updates.

content.full_text: The Department of Administrative Reforms & Public Grievances (DARPG) under the Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances & Pensions has approved the establishment of a Project Monitoring Unit (PMU) with the Quality Council of India (QCI) to implement recommendations from the NeSDA 2021 report. The PMU will work with DARPG for one year with a team of 3 QCI professionals to monitor and evaluate the implementation of e-Governance recommendations across States, UTs, and Central Governments. The primary objectives include achieving saturation of all 56 mandatory e-services, promoting integrated centralized portals, and improving scores across all seven NeSDA assessment parameters. The scope of work encompasses monitoring the NeSDA portal, coordinating with stakeholders, analyzing uploaded data, facilitating portal updates, and generating regular reports on e-service delivery, mandatory services, integrated portals, and assessment scores. The team will identify implementation bottlenecks and disseminate best practices. Professionals should have relevant qualifications, with at least one member having core expertise in data analytics from a Tier 1 Indian or international college.
